finding_results.notes: All three cited aphoristic turns now include audible speech friction. Line 87: 'There is no form for this situation' replaced with a restart ('At work — at work'), factual precision detour (listing insurance form categories including 'acts of God, which is a real category'), false start ('there is not a —'), and verbal stumble ('I don't know what you file for this'). Line 97: 'The things with paperwork, I know how to fight for. The other things, I usually notice after' — balanced chiastic structure broken into groping declaratives with verbal stumble ('The other things I — I notice those after') and less compressed ending ('A long time after, usually'). Line 109: 'I know I don't say it, period' replaced with groping repetition that shows the realization arriving in real time ('I don't think I say it. I don't think I have said it'); 'I'm saying it badly and late and in front of all these people' replaced with false start and concrete stumble ('I know this is not the — this is bad timing and probably the wrong room for it'). All revisions preserve the dramatic information while adding self-correction, restart, unnecessary precision, or verbal stumble. Doug's style bible constraints honored: factual precision under stress, moderate contractions, rare significant trailing-off, low repetition tolerance (doubled lines signal deep feeling).

summary: Single finding addressed: Doug's three key aphoristic toast lines roughened with speech friction (restarts, factual detours, verbal stumbles, self-corrections) while preserving their dramatic content. No changes to non-cited dialogue or narration. Heading contract preserved. Continuity checked against sheet — no contradictions introduced.
